events.title: Tranmere–Stanlow pipeline spill releases crude oil into the Mersey Estuary (UK)
events.description: A fracture/leak in a crude-oil pipeline between Tranmere and the Stanlow refinery resulted in an oil release into the River Mersey/Mersey Estuary. Response actions referenced include dispersant use, vacuum tanker recovery, and shoreline cleanup decisions for sensitive habitats.

location.name: Mersey Estuary / River Mersey (near Tranmere and Stanlow), North West England, UK

sources.headline: ScienceDirect abstract page (Marine Pollution Bulletin article)

events.title: Hurricane Chantal impacts Upper Texas Coast and moves inland across Texas
events.description: Hurricane Chantal impacted the Upper Texas Coast with landfall near High Island, Texas in early August 1989. Sources describe heavy rainfall and associated flooding impacts as the system moved inland across Texas.

location.name: High Island, Texas, United States (landfall area)

events.title: Hurricane Dean passes very near Bermuda and then moves toward Atlantic Canada
events.description: Reports indicate Hurricane Dean brought gale-force winds and rough seas as it passed very near Bermuda around August 6, 1989. The storm then tracked northward over the Northwest Atlantic toward Atlantic Canada.

sources.headline: Gale-force winds strike Bermuda as Dean nears

events.title: Typhoon Mac crosses Japan, causing flooding and deaths
events.description: A contemporaneous report says Typhoon Mac cut across Japan, bringing heavy rain and flooding that affected homes and caused fatalities. Digital Typhoon provides a storm summary for Typhoon Mac (1989).

location.name: Japan (storm impacts; reported in Chiba area)

sources.headline: Typhoon cuts deadly swath across Japan

events.title: Tropical Storm Roger (Typhoon 198917) brings heavy rain and flooding across Japan
events.description: Digital Typhoon summarizes Typhoon 198917 (Roger) as a system affecting Japan in late August 1989. The storm is associated with widespread rainfall impacts along its track through Japan.

events.title: Hurricane Kiko makes landfall in Baja California Sur, Mexico
events.description: Hurricane Kiko made landfall in Baja California Sur in late August 1989. NOAA sources summarize heavy rainfall associated with the storm and provide an archive storm wallet for the event.

location.name: Punta Arena area, Baja California Sur, Mexico

dedupe.cluster_hint: Hurricane Kiko (1989) Baja California Sur landfall

events.title: West Papua (Irian Jaya), Indonesia earthquake triggered landslides and mudslides
events.description: An earthquake struck Indonesia's then-Irian Jaya (West Papua) region on August 1, 1989. Reports indicate landslides and mudslides buried villages in the Kurima District area, causing fatalities and injuries.

location.name: Kurima District (Yahukimo Regency), Highland Papua (then Irian Jaya), Indonesia

sources.headline: Indonesia: Earthquake Aug 1989 - UNDRO Information Report No. 1

key_quotes.quote: UNDRO Information Report No. 1 ... earthquake ... landslides/mudslides ... buried villages.

events.title: Earthquake near Groznyy, Chechnya (USSR) reportedly caused damage and a fatality
events.description: An earthquake occurred in the Groznyy area of Chechnya on August 3, 1989. Catalog entries and reports referenced in the linked event pages indicate damage in the area and at least one reported death.

location.name: Groznyy area, Chechnya (then within the USSR)

key_quotes.quote: Event pages list damage reports for the Groznyy area and casualties (as reported).

events.title: Offshore earthquake near Hualien, Taiwan (approx. M6.6)
events.description: A strong earthquake occurred offshore to the southeast of Hualien, Taiwan on August 3, 1989. The linked catalogs list the event at approximately magnitude 6.6.

key_quotes.quote: Catalog entries list an offshore event near Hualien with magnitude around 6.6.

events.title: Lake Elsman area earthquake in the Santa Cruz Mountains, California
events.description: An earthquake struck near Lake Elsman in the Santa Cruz Mountains of California on August 8, 1989. Contemporary reporting and USGS materials describe minor damage and a death associated with the event.

location.name: Santa Cruz Mountains near Lake Elsman (between San Jose and Santa Cruz), California, USA

key_quotes.quote: Reporting described a quake near Lake Elsman with minor damage and a death.

events.title: Offshore Taranaki, New Zealand earthquake with minor damage reported
events.description: An earthquake occurred offshore of the Taranaki region of New Zealand on August 8, 1989. Linked records describe minor damage being reported.

key_quotes.quote: GeoNet record lists an offshore Taranaki event on 8 Aug 1989; other catalogs note minor damage.

events.title: Lowman wildfire in Boise National Forest contained after rain
events.description: Firefighters contained the Lowman wildfire in Boise National Forest after rain and drizzle helped slow the blaze. Reporting described the fire as about 46,500 acres and noted destruction of homes and a resort lodge, with extensive vegetation loss raising concerns about post-fire landslides.

location.name: Boise National Forest near Lowman, Idaho, USA

key_quotes.quote: Nation's largest wildfire was contained Tuesday after rain and drizzle soaked the Boise National Forest.

events.title: Lowman Complex fires threaten structures near Lowman, Idaho
events.description: A situation report stated that the Lowman Complex fires in Boise National Forest continued to threaten structures along the South Fork of the Payette River east of Lowman. Structural protection measures and additional evacuations were reported as ongoing.

location.name: Boise National Forest (Lowman area), Idaho, USA

key_quotes.quote: Lowman Complex ... continued to threaten structures along the South Fork of the Payette River east of Lowman.

events.title: Cleveland National Forest wildfire threatens Palomar Observatory area
events.description: A wildfire in the Cleveland National Forest near Palomar Mountain burned through steep brush under hot, dry conditions. Reporting said firefighting resources were positioned to protect homes and the Palomar Observatory area, and visitors were evacuated from nearby camps as ash posed concerns for sensitive equipment.

location.name: Cleveland National Forest (Palomar Mountain area), California, USA

sources.headline: Cleveland National Forest wildfire near Mt. Palomar threatens observatory; evacuations of visitors from camps

key_quotes.quote: An 11,000-acre wildfire ... threatened the Palomar Observatory ... visitors were evacuated from nearby camps.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1989 Palomar/Cleveland National Forest fire (San Diego/Riverside, CA)

events.title: Cleveland National Forest wildfire reported fully contained after 10 days
events.description: A wildfire in the Cleveland National Forest spanning northern San Diego and Riverside counties was reported fully contained after roughly 10 days of firefighting. Coverage said the fire burned 15,643 acres, threatened the Palomar Mountain Observatory, and affected habitat including parts of a natural preserve, with lingering smoke and closures reported.

location.name: Cleveland National Forest (northern San Diego and Riverside counties), California, USA

sources.headline: Cleveland National Forest wildfire (San Diego/Riverside counties, California) fully contained after 10 days

key_quotes.quote: The blaze, which charred 15,643 acres ... was fully contained late Monday after a 10-day battle.

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1989 Palomar/Cleveland National Forest fire (San Diego/Riverside, CA)

events.title: Foolhen Complex and Dollar-Needles fires in Payette National Forest reported contained
events.description: Cooler temperatures and scattered rain helped fire managers contain several major Idaho wildfires, including the Foolhen Complex and the Dollar-Needles Fire in Payette National Forest. Reporting said the fires were declared contained Sunday night with mop-up and rehabilitation efforts underway.

key_quotes.quote: Cooler temperatures and scattered rain helped contain several major Idaho wildfires.

events.title: EPA final rule delays certain RCRA Subtitle C hazardous-waste unit closure to allow limited non-hazardous receipt
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a final rule amending RCRA Subtitle C closure requirements. Under specified conditions, certain hazardous-waste management units could remain open after final receipt of hazardous waste in order to receive non-hazardous waste.

sources.headline: Federal Register notice: Final rule amendments for delay of closure period for hazardous waste management facilities
Rule checklists and applications for state authorization under RCRA
List of Federal Register notices pertaining to hazardous waste

key_quotes.quote: final rule amending ... closure requirements ... allow ... units to remain open ... to receive non-hazardous waste

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA RCRA closure delay final rule (1989-08-14)

events.title: EPA NPL rulemaking support materials reference Aug 16, 1989 Federal Register notice proposing two ATSDR sites (54 FR 33846)
events.description: EPA National Priorities List (NPL) rulemaking support materials include a referenced Federal Register notice dated August 16, 1989 (54 FR 33846). The referenced notice is described as proposing two ATSDR sites as part of NPL-related actions.

sources.headline: NPL rulemaking support material (NEPIS text record) referencing 54 FR 33846
NPL rulemaking support material (NEPIS text record) referencing 54 FR 33846

organizations: Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry (ATSDR)

key_quotes.quote: Federal Register notice dated August 16, 1989 (54 FR 33846) ... proposing two ATSDR sites

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA NPL support materials reference 54 FR 33846 (1989-08-16)
